notes: Proxy is required for same-origin /api requests when running the frontend on :5173 and backend on :4000; avoids CORS and index.html fallbacks.
Plugin may be @vitejs/plugin-react or @vitejs/plugin-react-swc depending on installed deps.
Client bootstrap for the Planar War web console + admin tools UI.
Vite entrypoint; renders <App/>.
Default behavior is same-origin (API_BASE_URL="") to work cleanly with Vite dev proxy and avoid CORS.
Set VITE_API_BASE_URL only when you intentionally want cross-origin calls (and have CORS configured).
Used by vendor admin pages (AdminVendorEconomyPage/AdminVendorAuditPage) and city-builder pages (MePage/OperationsPage).
Caution: keep secrets out of repo .env; Vite only exposes VITE_* at runtime, but secrets in-repo are still a security risk.
Now attaches Authorization: Bearer <token> automatically if localStorage pw_auth_v1 is present (shared auth across MUD/CityBuilder).
Adds helpers for CityBuilder city bootstrap/rename endpoints and extends fetchMe() payload metadata for hasCity/canCreateCity flow.
Adds typed public infrastructure DTOs and fetchPublicInfrastructureStatus(serviceMode) for city-builder lane/quote previews.
startTech() now accepts optional serviceMode so research can use private or NPC public lanes without raw ad-hoc payloads.
Adds typed PublicPressureSource DTOs and includes pressureSources in public infrastructure status/quote payloads.
Adds typed CityMudBridge DTOs plus fetchCityMudBridgeStatus() so the frontend can preview city→world support posture and exportable surplus.
Adds typed City ↔ MUD bridge consumer DTOs so frontend panels can render vendor/mission/civic guidance instead of only raw hook scores.
Adds typed mission-board DTOs plus fetchMissionBoard(), startMission(), and completeMission() so mission offers can surface bridge-aware support guidance in the UI.
Adds typed City ↔ MUD vendor policy DTOs so admin/vendor tooling can consume stock/price/cadence recommendations from the bridge status endpoint.
Admin vendor economy consumers may now receive bridge_runtime_effect preview fields on item rows from /api/admin/vendor_economy/items.
Admin vendor economy flows may now call /api/admin/vendor_economy/bridge_runtime_guarded to preview/apply server-guarded runtime changes for selected rows.
Adds typed vendor scenario replay/report DTOs plus fetchVendorScenarioReports() for the admin vendor economy reporting surface.
Adds vendor scenario export URL builder plus typed grouped review buckets so the admin UI can download CSV/JSON review packs without rebuilding query strings ad hoc.
provides: Typed DTOs for city-builder UI (MeProfile, missions, armies, heroes, tech, etc.)
fetchMe(), startTech(), api(path, init) with JSON/HTML guardrails

Uses same-origin /api requests (dev via Vite proxy; prod via reverse proxy / same host).
Endpoints: GET /api/admin/spawn_points?..., POST /api/admin/spawn_points (create), PUT /api/admin/spawn_points/:id, bulk_delete, bulk_move, clone, scatter.
Mother Brain endpoints: GET /api/admin/spawn_points/mother_brain/status, POST /api/admin/spawn_points/mother_brain/wave, POST /api/admin/spawn_points/mother_brain/wipe.
Town Baseline endpoints: POST /api/admin/spawn_points/town_baseline/plan and /api/admin/spawn_points/town_baseline/apply.
Handles Mother Brain destructive confirm_required (HTTP 409) flow: displays expectedConfirmToken and requires re-run with confirm token for commit when deletions would occur.
Adds client-side quick search to narrow the loaded list without re-querying the server.
Shows compact summary panels for wave/wipe/baseline results; raw JSON is tucked into <details> for sanity.
Displays confirm token both when required (HTTP 409 confirm_required) and when merely available for later commit.
Mother Brain ops UX: supports confirm_required flow with copy/fill token controls and per-parameter token scoping (token resets when inputs change).
Wipe confirm adds an extra human-speed lock: type 'WIPE' before token entry/commit enables.
Adds diff preview panel for Mother Brain wave/wipe (delete/insert/update/skip + export JSON).
UI renders opsPreview diff previews for Mother Brain wave/wipe AND Town baseline plan/apply (with JSON export).
Snapshot/Restore endpoints: POST /api/admin/spawn_points/snapshot, POST /api/admin/spawn_points/restore.
Server snapshot endpoints: GET /api/admin/spawn_points/snapshots, POST /api/admin/spawn_points/snapshots/save, GET/DELETE /api/admin/spawn_points/snapshots/:id
Restore safety UI: shows high-risk restore warning and requires confirm phrase 'RESTORE' for cross-shard and/or brain-owned restores, plus confirm token for destructive updates.
P2–P3: Server snapshots UI: save/load/delete spawn snapshot docs stored on web-backend.
Snapshot save supports tags (comma list) + notes; list supports sort + tag filter + search via query params.
Scatter tool uses server-provided protoId datalist (/api/admin/spawn_points/proto_options) with name+id labels and manual fallback.
P2–P3: Server snapshots UI: save/load/delete spawn snapshot docs stored on web-backend.
Snapshot save supports tags (comma list) + notes; list supports sort + tag filter + search via query params.
Scatter tool uses server-provided protoId datalist (/api/admin/spawn_points/proto_options) with name+id labels and manual fallback.
Scatter tool adds quick-pick chips (common protoIds) to reduce typos and speed placement; manual entry still allowed.
Region filter adds quick-pick chips (common regionIds) to speed filtering without scrolling/typing.

v0.4 also consumes city↔MUD bridge vendorPolicy to preview recommended stock/price/cadence posture while tuning vendor_item_economy rows.
v0.5 stages and applies live bridge recommendations per row or across visible vendor items, turning policy preview into actionable tuning.
v0.6 surfaces per-row bridge runtime previews (effective stock/cadence/price + fill-state) and allows staging those live runtime knobs directly into the admin editor.
v0.7 adds preview/apply controls for server-guarded runtime changes so admins can stage or commit softened bridge-driven vendor updates without bypassing safety rails.
v0.8 adds lane-aware vendor posture display and filtering so operators can stage/apply bridge effects by essentials, comfort, luxury, or arcane stock lanes.
v0.9 adds explicit guarded lane-set controls and preview/apply actions so operators can target essentials/comfort/luxury/arcane groups directly instead of relying only on visible-row filtering.
v1.0 adds guarded preset preview/apply controls so operators can run audited vendor posture presets on top of lane-set targeting instead of rebuilding the same selections by hand.
v1.1 surfaces recent guarded runtime scenario entries so preset/lane previews and applies are visible as an auditable operator timeline instead of disappearing into toast fog.
v1.2 upgrades the guarded runtime timeline into a filterable replay/report panel with rollup chips, scope controls, and expandable scenario details (warnings + sample rows).
v1.3 adds export buttons plus grouped preset/lane/bridge/state review cards so operators can review the last posture window without reading raw logs.
Uses same-origin /api requests (dev via Vite proxy; prod via reverse proxy / same host).
Not currently routed from App.tsx.
Now acts as the city bootstrap funnel when an authenticated account has no city yet, and exposes authenticated city rename controls.

Now renders mission board cards that surface bridge-derived support guidance and allows start/complete flows from the same dashboard.
Page shell now delegates public infrastructure, bridge, city core, and policy/army sections to dedicated city components while preserving runtime behavior.
Page shell now delegates fetch/refresh loops, flash notices, and city action handler wiring to a dedicated controller hook while preserving runtime behavior.

App.tsx is large; keep known-good commits before major UI polish passes to avoid JSX cascade errors.
